//pin: The harness:<family> label is the tier-1 per-bead harness pin (agentevents_hqwn59.go).
//pin2: model_pin records the harmonik-controlled model the model_selected event must carry
//pin3: (empty for codex — model is not harmonik-controlled; set by $CODEX_HOME/config.toml).
//branch: target_branch (optional, D2): when set, core-loop-seed.sh creates/resets that
//branch2: branch off main in the SCRATCH clone BEFORE creating the bead, and appends a
//branch3: ## Branching fenced-yaml block (target_branch: <b>) to the bead description so
//branch4: the daemon lands the task there instead of main (BI-009b). The runner git-
//branch5: verifies the landing (the workspace_merge_status event is never emitted).

seeds.title: core-loop-proof seed: trivial one-line change (codex)
seeds.body: Append a single dated line to docs/core-loop-proof-seed.txt and commit. No other change. This is a fixture bead that exercises the real task-processing loop end to end.

seeds.title: core-loop-proof seed: trivial one-line change (pi, single)
seeds.body: Append a single dated line to docs/core-loop-proof-seed.txt and commit. No other change. Fixture bead for the pi harness family (single workflow mode).

seeds.title: core-loop-proof seed: two-step review round-trip (pi, dot)
seeds.body:
Goal: the file docs/core-loop-proof-seed.txt must end up with TWO new appended lines — one containing the token LINE-A and one containing the token LINE-B — added in TWO SEPARATE commits, one per implementation pass. Do not modify any other file.

DECIDE WHICH PASS YOU ARE ON by reading docs/core-loop-proof-seed.txt right now:
- If the file does NOT yet contain a line with the token LINE-A: this is the FIRST pass. Append exactly ONE new line whose content is the token LINE-A, then commit. Add ONLY LINE-A on this pass; do NOT add LINE-B yet.
- If the file ALREADY contains a line with the token LINE-A but does NOT contain a line with the token LINE-B: this is the SECOND pass (the reviewer asked for changes). Append exactly ONE new line whose content is the token LINE-B, then commit. Adding this LINE-B line and committing it is REQUIRED — the run FAILS if this pass makes no commit, so you MUST append LINE-B and commit; do not report the task complete without it.

(The reviewer feedback delivered to you between passes will also ask for LINE-B.)

## Review

Apply this acceptance rubric to docs/core-loop-proof-seed.txt as it stands after the implementer's latest commit:

- If the file does NOT contain a line with the token LINE-B, the change is INCOMPLETE. Return verdict REQUEST_CHANGES and say in your notes that a line containing the token LINE-B must be added on the next pass.
- Only when the file contains BOTH a line with the token LINE-A AND a line with the token LINE-B, return verdict APPROVE.
seeds.//dot: dot:review-loop pins this bead's workflow FILE to the scratch-root review-loop.dot
seeds.//dot2: (resolveWorkflowRef tier-1). Required for same-model: the project-default
seeds.//dot3: workflow.dot is committed as standard-bead.dot, whose review node pins
seeds.//dot4: harness=claude-code + model=claude-opus-4-8 (a claude leak), and every landing's
seeds.//dot5: `git reset --hard HEAD` restores it, so overriding that tracked file cannot hold.

seeds.title: core-loop-proof seed: trivial one-line change (claude)
seeds.body: Append a single dated line to docs/core-loop-proof-seed.txt and commit. No other change. Fixture bead for the claude-code harness family (flag-gated cell).
